items.title: As AI Adoption Rises, Trust in AI Results Declines Among Americans
items.summary: A new poll reveals that while more Americans are adopting AI tools, a growing number express distrust in the accuracy and reliability of the results generated by these technologies.

items.title: 15% of Americans Open to Working for an AI Boss, New Poll Shows
items.summary: A recent Quinnipiac poll indicates that a significant minority of Americans, 15%, are willing to work under an AI supervisor, highlighting evolving attitudes towards AI integration in the workplace.

items.title: Out of Sight but Not Out of Mind: Hybrid Memory for Dynamic Video World Models
items.summary: Researchers introduce a novel hybrid memory system designed to enhance dynamic video world models, allowing AI to better understand and predict complex, evolving visual environments over longer durations.

items.title: ShotStream: Streaming Multi-Shot Video Generation for Interactive Storytelling
items.summary: A new research paper unveils ShotStream, a system capable of generating multi-shot videos in a streaming fashion, paving the way for more interactive and dynamic AI-powered storytelling experiences.

items.title: LiteLLM, a Popular AI Gateway Startup, Parts Ways with Controversial Delve
items.summary: AI gateway startup LiteLLM has announced its separation from Delve, a move that could impact its standing and partnerships within the rapidly evolving AI ecosystem.

items.title: OpenAI Aids Disaster Response Teams in Asia with AI Solutions
items.summary: OpenAI is leveraging its AI technology to assist disaster response teams across Asia, helping them translate AI insights into actionable strategies during critical situations.

items.title: I Built a Local AI Agent That Audits My Own Articles. It Flagged Every Single One.
items.summary: A developer recounts building a local AI agent to audit their articles, discovering its surprising effectiveness in flagging issues across all their work, highlighting the potential of personalized AI assistants.

items.title: Goose Offers Free Alternative to Claude Code's $200/Month Service
items.summary: A new tool named Goose is gaining attention for providing similar code generation capabilities to Claude Code, but at no cost, potentially disrupting the market for AI coding assistants.

items.title: PackForcing: Short Video Training Suffices for Long Video Sampling and Long Context Inference
items.summary: New research introduces PackForcing, a method demonstrating that training AI models on short video segments can effectively enable them to generate and infer from much longer video contexts, improving efficiency.

items.title: MedOpenClaw: Auditable Medical Imaging Agents Reasoning over Uncurated Full Studies
items.summary: MedOpenClaw introduces auditable AI agents for medical imaging that can reason over complete, uncurated studies, promising greater transparency and reliability in clinical applications.

items.title: Long-Horizon Agents Are Here, But Full Autopilot Remains Elusive
items.summary: While AI agents are demonstrating capabilities for long-horizon tasks, experts caution that true full autopilot systems are still a distant reality, emphasizing the ongoing challenges in complex autonomous decision-making.

items.title: The Creator of Claude Code Reveals Workflow, Captivating Developers
items.summary: The developer behind Claude Code has shared their innovative workflow, sparking significant interest and discussion among the developer community eager to learn advanced AI integration techniques.

items.title: Trace2Skill: Distilling Trajectory-Local Lessons into Transferable Agent Skills
items.summary: Researchers present Trace2Skill, a method that distills specific lessons from agent trajectories into broadly transferable skills, enhancing the efficiency and adaptability of AI agents in various tasks.

items.title: RealChart2Code: Advancing Chart-to-Code Generation with Real Data and Multi-Task Evaluation
items.summary: RealChart2Code proposes a new benchmark and approach for generating code from charts using real-world data and multi-task evaluation, significantly improving the accuracy and utility of this AI capability.

items.title: What is OpenClaw? A Self-Hosted AI Assistant System
items.summary: OpenClaw is introduced as a self-hosted AI assistant system, offering users greater control and privacy over their AI interactions and data.
